hypothesis: dropout_head=0.1 sweep from #47 (p=0.3). #47 produced the loop's first balanced-prediction regime (sens 0.388 / spec 0.383) at p=0.3, but regressed metric to 0.3855. At p=0 (#37) the model fully parks at sens 0.765 / spec 0.217 (metric 0.4910). p=0.1 probes the midpoint: if dropout's mechanism-distinct break on co-adaptation interpolates smoothly, p=0.1 should produce a regime between those two — and potentially a metric above both if the sweet spot lives in [0.05, 0.15]. Three clean outcomes: (a) metric > 0.4910 with partially balanced sens/spec — genuine corridor-escape discrimination win; (b) interpolated metric between #47 and #37 — linear response, further fine-tuning needed; (c) regression below #37 — dropout is always net-negative at this scale.
